summary: Gruber argues that Truth Social isn't really a social network at all — it's effectively Trump's personal blog. Despite a 400% spike in users during the Trump-Musk feud, Truth Social has only 1% of X's active US mobile users, and virtually no one of consequence besides Trump himself posts there. Gruber notes that even Trump's own administration officials post primarily on X, not Truth Social. The fact that Trump never required his allies to migrate to Truth Social reveals it was never meant to compete with X. It functions as a one-way broadcast platform for Trump's pronouncements, which then get amplified by TV news.
key_points: Truth Social has roughly 1% of X's US mobile users and no prominent users besides Trump himself — even his own administration posts primarily on X

The Trump-Musk alliance was inherently contradictory since Musk's X and Trump's Truth Social were ostensibly competitors, yet this tension never surfaced because Truth Social was never a real social network
Trump never required his officials to stop posting on X and use Truth Social, which reveals its true purpose as a personal broadcast channel
Truth Social's other users are essentially blog commenters shitposting in hopes of getting Trump's attention
The platform is exceedingly successful when judged as what it actually is: a megaphone for Trump's pronouncements that get read aloud on TV news
money_quotes: Truth Social is essentially just Trump's blog. Truth Social is exceedingly unpopular when judged as a social network; but it's exceedingly successful as a blog.
Nobody of note other than Trump himself uses Truth Social.
All the other people using Truth Social are effectively reading his blog and shitposting comments and memes in response to his posts, trying to get his attention.

I've never once, ever, seen a post from anyone else on Truth Social make the news. Trump is not just the one and only person of consequence using it, his is the one and only account on Truth Social that you ever, ever hear about.
Truth Social is functioning as intended: it's just an outlet for Trump to spew his demented mad-king musings and, most importantly, get some of his all-caps-laden bangers read aloud on the TV news.
For all the pathetic obsequiousness of every single lickspittle official serving in the Trump 2.0 administration, none of them are even vaguely active on Truth Social.
Long before this easily-predictable breakup of two unstable sociopathic egomaniacs (and who would be genuinely surprised if Musk is back in the Oval Office next week?).
key_insight: Truth Social was never meant to be a competitive social network — it's a one-man broadcast platform functioning as Trump's personal blog, and its success should be measured on those terms.
